chunk_summary: This section discusses neurodevelopmental screening and psychosocial support in stunted children, emphasizing age-specific assessments, nurturing care, caregiver wellbeing, and referral pathways for developmental and growth continuity.
learning_objectives: Understand the link between stunting and neurodevelopmental vulnerability across age groups.
Learn age-specific screening methods for developmental milestones and psychosocial factors.
Explore nurturing care strategies and caregiver support as determinants of child growth and development.
Familiarize with India-compatible referral pathways like RBSK and DEIC for developmental interventions.
key_facts: Stunting is associated with developmental vulnerability and height deficits.

Screening should include age-specific factors: under 3 years (hearing, vision, motor skills), 3–5 years (language, behavior), school-age (attention, fatigue, sleep), adolescents (mood, body image, menstrual history).
Trivandrum Developmental Screening Chart (TDSC) is recommended for quick assessments under 3 years.
RBSK program enables screening and referral for children aged 0–18 years through District Early Intervention Centers (DEIC).
Caregiver stress and depressive symptoms should be addressed as growth determinants.
reference_quote: The RBSK program enables screening and referral across 0–18 years through District Early Intervention Centers (DEIC).
document_key_topics: Referral pathways for growth and developmental interventions
